app.name: Jujutsu (jj)
app.one_liner: A Git-compatible version control system that is both simple and powerful, featuring change-centric workflows and pluggable storage backends.

screens.name: Main CLI/TUI Interface
screens.purpose: Provide access to core version control operations including commit manipulation, bookmark management, and history navigation.
primary_actions: Execute `jj bookmark advance` to auto-move bookmarks
Run `jj arrange` to reorder or abandon revisions via TUI
View and restore historical or divergent changes using disambiguation syntax

rust_commands.name: jj_arrange
rust_commands.purpose: Launch a terminal user interface to interactively reorder, split, or abandon revisions.

rust_commands.name: jj_bookmark_advance
rust_commands.purpose: Automatically move bookmarks forward to a target revision using configurable revset policies.

rust_commands.name: jj_restore_from_divergent
rust_commands.purpose: Restore a change to a previous version using disambiguated change ID syntax (e.g., xyz/1).

mvp_plan: week 1: Build basic `jj arrange` TUI for revision reordering
week 1: Implement core change tracking with external bookmark storage
week 1: Integrate Git backend for commit/file persistence
acceptance_tests: Confirm `jj bookmark advance` moves local bookmarks forward according to configured revsets.
Ensure remote names containing whitespace do not cause panics during fetch/push operations.
Validate that change IDs with divergence syntax (e.g., xyz/1) correctly resolve to historical versions.
Verify that `jj arrange` launches a functional TUI allowing revision reordering and abandonment.
